title: Alvo Project Descriptor
description: Declarative definition of an Alvo backend. One artifact for every path: Docker mount, `alvo apply`, Management API, AddAlvo().FromDescriptor(), and admin UI export. Conditions (rules, hook/automation conditions) use a CEL subset; payload transformations use JSONata. Env/secrets (connection strings, admin credentials) do NOT belong here — the descriptor defines the backend, not the infrastructure configuration.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema; ignored by Alvo.

apiVersion.description: Descriptor format version. Self-describing so a loader can dispatch the right parser before fetching the schema. Within v1 the format evolves additively only; a breaking change becomes alvo.dev/v2 at a new URL.

name.description: Project identifier (kebab-case). In standalone mode it identifies the project within the instance.

revision.description: Content revision counter, incremented on every applied change; used for optimistic concurrency during apply. NOT the format version (that is apiVersion).

tenancy.description: Declares this backend as multi-tenant (definition, not infrastructure). Tenant resolution (subdomain/header/claim) is host/env config, never here. When enabled, entities default to tenancy:scoped unless they opt out with tenancy:global.

enabled.description: Turn on row-level multi-tenancy (shared DB + shared schema, discriminator column).

dynamicEntities.description: Governance for runtime, user-defined entities (the dynamic schema-registry driver). End-users create record types at runtime via the Management API into metadata tables — they are NOT declared under `entities`. This block is how the host expresses the security policy and limits over that whole class. The store itself is F7.

enabled.description: Allow end-users to define entities at runtime.

namePrefix.description: Reserved name prefix for runtime-created entities, so they can never collide with descriptor-defined entity names.

defaultRules.description: Authorization rules applied to EVERY runtime-created entity. A virtual entity must carry policy exactly as a physical one carries `rules`; without this, default-deny makes user entities unreachable (or, if defaulted open, insecure). Missing operation = deny.

allowedFieldTypes.description: The subset of field types end-users may use on runtime entities.

maxFieldsPerEntity.description: Per-entity field quota for runtime entities.

maxRecordsPerEntity.description: Per-entity record quota for runtime entities (per tenant).

maxEntitiesPerTenant.description: How many runtime entities a single tenant may create.

providers.description: Enabled identity providers. `local` = credentials managed by Alvo (ASP.NET Core Identity). `oidc` = a generic OIDC relying party — this is the path for a custom / self-hosted identity provider (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, Entra ID, …); its endpoints are infra/env, never here.

roles.description: Application roles beyond the built-in ones (anon, authenticated, admin). Referenced in CEL via @user.role.

admin.description: Admin dashboard configuration — definition ONLY (access mapping, branding). Enablement, path, IP allowlist and the script UI-edit gate are env.

access.description: Mapping to dashboard access levels via CEL expressions over @user.

title.description: Dashboard title shown in the admin UI.

logoUrl.description: URL or path to the dashboard logo.

entities.description: Entity definitions. Key = entity name (snake_case, plural recommended). Each entity auto-generates a CRUD API, validation, and a schema registry record. The name `users` is reserved (the built-in auth entity).

automation.description: ECA rules (event–condition–action). Key = rule name. Run post-commit from the outbox, durable, with retries.

templates.description: Reusable message templates referenced by email/notification actions. Key = template name.

webhooks.description: Managed webhook endpoints (Standard Webhooks: HMAC signing, retries, DLQ).

endpoints.description: Managed webhook endpoints, keyed by endpoint name.

secretRef.description: Secret name in ISecretStore (never the secret value itself).

functions.description: Custom logic — csx scripts (standalone). Key = function name. Trust: admin-level code.

script.description: Relative path to a .csx file inside the descriptor bundle.

trigger.description: Optional trigger; a function without a trigger is only invocable from the `function` automation action.

http.description: Expose the function at an HTTP route (also serves as an inbound receiver).

method.description: HTTP method (defaults to POST when omitted).

execution.description: Sync = in the request path (short timeout); queued = via outbox and worker (default).

^x-.description: Extension keys. Alvo ignores these but guarantees passthrough through apply -> export. Use for host/tooling metadata (UI hints, provenance).

cel.description: Condition in the CEL subset. Context: @user, @tenant; hooks/events additionally expose old, new, changed(field). Compiled fail-fast at apply time.

celExpr.description: A tagged CEL expression in a value position, distinguishing it unambiguously from a JSON literal.

valueOrExpr.description: A literal JSON value, or a tagged CEL expression object {"$cel": "..."}.

boolOrCel.description: A static boolean, or a CEL expression over @user for a conditional (e.g. per-role) decision.

jsonata.description: JSONata transformation expression (JSON -> JSON). Runs strictly post-commit (after-side), with depth/time limits. {{...}} templates are syntactic sugar.

eventPattern.description: Event pattern, e.g. entity.deals.updated, entity.*.created, entity.orders.* or the coalesced batch shape entity.orders.created.batch.

rules.description: Per-operation authorization rules — CEL, compiled into a SQL predicate. Missing operation = deny (secure-by-default).

description.description: Human-readable description of the entity (surfaced to agents and in the admin UI).

renamedFrom.description: Previous name of this entity: declares a rename so apply preserves data instead of drop+add. Safe to leave permanently; ignored once the old name no longer exists.

storage.description: physical = a real table (introspected/migrated); dynamic = the shared metadata-driven store (no DDL, instant apply). Everything above the registry treats both identically.

tenancy.description: scoped = rows carry tenant_id and are isolated per tenant (default when tenancy.enabled); global = shared reference data (číselník) visible to all tenants.

softDelete.description: Framework-managed soft delete: a managed deleted_at column, DELETE becomes a soft delete, and reads/list/get/rollup auto-exclude soft-deleted rows. A restore operation is provided.

audit.description: Inject framework-managed audit columns: created_at, created_by, updated_at, updated_by.

fields.description: Entity fields. The `id` field (uuid, PK) is added automatically when not defined.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks. before* = in-transaction, no network, may reject/mutate; after* = post-commit from the outbox.

realtime.description: Publish changes over the realtime channel (with subject-level authz).

indexes.description: Explicit composite/extra indexes beyond the automatic ones (PK, unique, ref).

fields.description: Fields covered by the index, in order.

unique.description: Whether the index enforces uniqueness across the covered fields.

description.description: Human-readable description of the field (surfaced to agents and in the admin UI).

renamedFrom.description: Previous name of this field: declares a rename so apply preserves data instead of drop+add.

required.description: Whether the field must be present (NOT NULL).

unique.description: Whether values must be unique across the entity.

nullable.description: Explicitly allow NULL; the default is derived from required.

default.description: Default value: a JSON literal, or a tagged expression {"$cel": "..."} (e.g. {"$cel": "now()"}, {"$cel": "gen_random_uuid()"}, {"$cel": "@user.id"}). The $cel default context includes @user/@tenant and is evaluated at insert time.

entity.description: type=ref only — target entity (FK to its id). May target the reserved `users` entity, or a dynamic/runtime entity (late-bound).

onDelete.description: type=ref only. Registry-level semantics: enforced as a DB FK for physical targets, app-enforced for dynamic targets.

validation.description: Optional CEL value validation (context: value, new).

index.description: Create an index (for dynamic entities = generated column + index over the JSON path).

hidden.description: Field is never returned in API responses. true = always hidden; a CEL expression over @user makes visibility conditional (per-role masking, e.g. "@user.role != 'compliance'").

readOnly.description: Field cannot be modified via the API. true = always read-only; a CEL expression over @user makes it conditional.

computed.description: Value derived from other fields of the SAME row (pure arithmetic/expression, deterministic, no side effects), e.g. unit_price * amount. May also reference this entity's rollup fields (the framework maintains the value). Field becomes read-only.

rollup.description: Value aggregated over RELATED records, transactionally consistent (e.g. sum of child line_total). Framework maintains it; the field is read-only.

from.description: Child entity that references this one.

op.description: Aggregate operation over the child records.

field.description: Field on the child entity to aggregate (required for all ops except count).

via.description: The FK field on the child entity that points back to this parent. Required when the child has more than one ref to this parent (e.g. follows.follower vs follows.followee).

where.description: Optional CEL filter on child records.

action.description: Before-actions run in-transaction: reject or mutate only. No network, no external calls.

reject.description: Cancels the operation; the text becomes the detail of the RFC 7807 error.

mutate.description: Payload patch before write: field -> literal value or tagged {"$cel": "..."} expression.

description.description: Human-readable description of the automation rule.

trigger.description: What fires the rule: an event pattern or a cron schedule.

delivery.description: perItem = one execution per affected row; batch = one execution with an array payload (coalesces bulk operations; matches the entity.*.*.batch event shape).

actions.description: Actions to run when the rule fires (in order).

template.description: A reusable message template. Provide an inline body or a bundle-relative bodyFile.

bodyFile.description: Relative path to a template file inside the descriptor bundle.

action.description: After-side action (post-commit, durable, retries). Payload transformations = JSONata; {{...}} is sugar.

recordId.description: Id or {{...}} template; omit to create a new record.

payload.description: Field -> literal value or tagged {"$cel": "..."} expression; {{...}} sugar allowed in string values.

headersSecretRef.description: Secret name in ISecretStore holding request headers (e.g. auth token); never the value itself.
